evidence.snippet: Vitiligo is an acquired cutaneous disorder of pigmentation... Recent data provide strong evidence supporting an autoimmune pathogenesis of vitiligo.

evidence.reference_title: Vitiligo in the City of Bukavu ( Democratic Republic of Congo).
evidence.snippet: Vitiligo is a skin disorder characterized by selective loss of melanocytes resulting in circumscribed, depigmented macules and patches... its effects can be psychological, leading to stigmatization and suicidal ideation.

evidence.reference_title: The definition and assessment of vitiligo: a consensus report of the Vitiligo European Task Force.
evidence.snippet: Vitiligo is the most common depigmenting disorder, which affects 0.5-1% of the worldwide population, causing disfigurement and serious disturbances in well being.

evidence.reference_title: Cumulative life course impairment in vitiligo.
evidence.snippet: Vitiligo is an acquired, idiopathic skin disease characterized by the mostly progressive loss of the inherited skin color leading to white patches... The disease burden includes stigmatization, depression, impaired quality of life, lack of self-confidence, embarrassment and self-consciousness.

evidence.reference_title: Cancer risks in patients with vitiligo: a Mendelian randomization study.
evidence.snippet: Vitiligo was causally associated with reduced risks of several cancers... melanoma (OR 0.9983; 95% CI 0.9976-0.9990; p < 0.001), non-melanoma skin cancer (OR 0.9997; 95% CI 0.9995-0.9999; p < 0.001).
